assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
Output out changes value on at least one observed clock edge after reset release.
cases.goal: Confirm that the LFSR makes observable progress without assuming a fixed number of cycles.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Enrichment adds explicit expectation of change rather than just defined values.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset and sample out over a few clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Sample on rising edge of clk; no fixed latency assumed.

expected_properties: After deasserting reset, the 4-bit output follows the deterministic LFSR sequence defined by the XOR feedback and inversion.
The output never enters an X or Z state during the observed window.
cases.goal: Validate that the LFSR produces the expected pseudo‑random sequence over several clock cycles.
notes: Sequence length is not assumed; observation window is limited to 8 cycles.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Reset has been released and the design is stable.
stimulus_intent: Release reset and allow the LFSR to advance for a fixed number of clock edges.
timing_assumptions: Observe on rising edge of clk; no assumption about exact latency beyond one cycle per shift.

expected_properties: A brief (one‑cycle) high pulse on rst forces the output to zero for that cycle and does not cause X propagation after deassertion.
cases.goal: Check robustness to a single‑cycle reset pulse (glitch).
notes: The pulse width is one clock cycle; longer pulses are covered by reset_001.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Design is in a known non‑zero state before the glitch.
stimulus_intent: Inject a one‑cycle high on rst while clk is running.
timing_assumptions: Glitch aligns with a rising edge of clk; observation continues for subsequent cycles.
